business.headline: Kotak Mahindra Bank MD and CEO Ashok Vaswani to step down at year-endKotak Mahindra Bank has begun searching for a successor after CEO Ashok Vaswani said he will not seek reappointment when his term ends in December 2026. The move comes as the lender seeks faster growth after regulatory scrutiny and may keep investors focused on leadership continuity.

NSE, Jio IPOs have retail investors excited. But are big listings worth the hype?NSE and Jio are already generating plenty of excitement. But before investors join the rush, recent IPO history offers an important lesson about hype, valuations and long-term returns. Read on to find out why big listings don't always translate into big gains.

Will life insurance pay after a fire? Here's what every family should knowRecent fire tragedies have triggered questions over whether life insurance pays after accidental deaths. Experts say standard term plans generally cover such deaths unless false disclosures or policy exclusions apply.

Asian markets crash, will Dalal Street feel the aftershock on Monday?From South Korea's Kospi to Japan's Nikkei, Asian stock markets have tumbled sharply, leaving investors on edge. As Dalal Street prepares to reopen after a three-day holiday on Monday, will the BSE and NSE also open deep in the red?

From ITR filing to TDS: 3 major tax deadlines in July 2026 you shouldn't missWhether you're a salaried employee, a business owner or an employer, keeping track of these dates can help you avoid last-minute stress. Here are three key tax deadlines you should not miss in July 2026.

Will your family get your EPF pension after your death? Here's what you need to knowNo one likes to think about what happens after they're gone. But if you're an EPF member, knowing whether your family can continue receiving your pension could make a big difference to their financial future.

Will DA rise before the 8th Pay Commission? Here's what the latest data suggestsThe wait for the 8th Pay Commission continues, but all eyes are now on the next DA revision. As inflation remains elevated and the July review draws closer, is another DA hike just around the corner?

Govt restores commercial LPG supply to pre-West Asia crisis levelsThe move comes after the government reviewed the country's fuel availability and found that both domestic production and imported LPG supplies have improved.

AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Tokyo's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

entertainment.headline: Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 2: Akshay's film crosses Rs 50 crore globally Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle has crossed the Rs 50 crore mark at the worldwide box office in just two days. The film collected Rs 20 crore nett on Saturday, taking its India nett total to Rs 39 crore and worldwide gross to Rs 57.50 crore.

Harry Styles collapses on stage after choking while drinking water during concert Harry Styles suffered a brief choking scare after accidentally choking while drinking water during his Wembley Stadium concert in London. He collapsed on stage for a few seconds before recovering.

Actor Akanksha Chamola confirms divorce from Gaurav Khanna on Lock Upp Season 2 Akanksha Chamola said on Lock Upp Season 2 that she and Gaurav Khanna have been living separately for a year and are headed for divorce. The actor's latest revelation has also renewed attention on the couple's earlier conversations about parenthood.

Actor Sonu Mishra quits Kala Hiran amid Salman Khan's legal battle Actor Sonu Mishra has stepped away from Kala Hiran: The Battle for Legacy, saying the film was designed to tarnish a senior actor's image. His exit comes as Salman Khan's legal challenge against the project continues.

Khushbu Sundar defends wedding post amid backlash after K Bhagyaraj's death Khushbu Sundar hit back at criticism over her wedding-day post after K Bhagyaraj's death. The reaction came as condolences and a final video from her daughter's Goa wedding spread online.

Suniel Shetty shares granddaughter Evaarah's daily ritual involving PM Modi's photo Suniel Shetty has shared an unusual daily ritual involving his 15-month-old granddaughter Evaarah and a photograph of Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Here's what the actor revealed and why he believes it is significant.

Five-time award-winning cinematographer Dillip Ray dies at 72 after brief illness Cinematographer Dillip Ray has died at 72. Read about his remarkable journey, celebrated films, award-winning career and the tributes pouring in after his death.

Ujjwal Nikam praises Rajkummar Rao for bringing his story to life in Prahaar Public Prosecutor Ujjwal Nikam has praised Rajkummar Rao and Avinash Arun for bringing his story to screen in Prahaar. He said the film captures landmark prosecutions as well as the private loneliness behind them.

Neeraj Ghaywan criticises TVF's Panchayat for featuring 'only upper-caste names' Filmmaker Neeraj Ghaywan has questioned the authenticity of TVF's Panchayat over its portrayal of village life. Here's what he said and why his remarks have sparked discussion.

Jackky Bhagnani's old dating app profile goes viral after situationship remarks Jackky Bhagnani is back in the spotlight after old screenshots of his alleged Raya profile resurfaced on Reddit, months after his "situationship" remark about wife Rakul Preet Singh sparked controversy.

Suniel Shetty on Don 3 row: Ranveer Singh brought Rs 3,500 crore to the industry Actor Suniel Shetty has come out in support of Ranveer Singh amid the Don 3 controversy, questioning calls to ban the actor and making a strong case for artistic freedom. Here's what he said.

Priyadarshan's Haiwaan starring Akshay Kumar and Saif Ali Khan gets release date Akshay Kumar and Saif Ali Khan's much-awaited reunion film Haiwaan has officially locked its theatrical release date. Here's everything revealed so far about Priyadarshan's upcoming thriller.

K Bhagyaraj: The screenplay king who became the voice of middle-class India Veteran filmmaker K Bhagyaraj, celebrated as Tamil cinema's King of Screenplay, died in Chennai on June 27 after a cardiac arrest. His death, coming just days after mentor Bharathiraja's death, marks another major loss for the industry.

K Bhagyaraj funeral: CM Vijay and Rajinikanth arrive to honour the filmmaker Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay and superstar Rajinikanth arrived to pay their final respects to veteran filmmaker K Bhagyaraj. The legendary director, who died at 73, is being accorded state honours for his immense contribution to Tamil cinema.

K Bhagyaraj death: Tamil Nadu announces state honours for legendary filmmaker The Tamil Nadu government, under Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay, announced state honours for veteran filmmaker K Bhagyaraj following his death at 73. Tributes poured in from across the film fraternity, with Venkatesh Daggubati, Kamal Haasan, MK Stalin, Raghava Lawrence, Selvaraghavan and Simran remembering his lasting legacy.

Mohanlal makes Germany dance to his tunes, grooves to Dhanush's Megham Karukatha Mohanlal turned a star-studded event in Germany into a memorable celebration as he sang and danced to popular tracks, including Dhanush's Megham Karukatha, Illuminati and Kalapakkara. Videos of the Malayalam superstar's energetic stage performance with Saniya Iyappan and KS Chithra have gone viral online.

The trailer trap and forgetting art of surprise: How much is too much? What makes a great movie trailer? Audiences, editors, filmmakers and trade experts discuss the fine balance between building curiosity and giving away too much.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram sequel is on, confirms writer Raj Nidimoru Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram is set to return with a sequel, writer Raj Nidimoru announced during the film's success meet in Vizag. The announcement comes after the Nandini Reddy directorial emerged as the highest-grossing women-led film and received widespread praise.

Veteran Tamil filmmaker, writer K Bhagyaraj dies of heart attack at 73 Veteran Tamil filmmaker, actor and screenwriter K Bhagyaraj died in Chennai at the age of 73 following a heart attack. His funeral is scheduled to be held at 1.30 pm at Chennai's Besant Nagar crematorium.

general news.headline: Will face hell: Iran's stark warning to US, Gulf nations amid escalating attacksIran launched missile and drone strikes on US military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain after fresh American attacks on Iranian targets. As tensions surged despite a fragile ceasefire, US President Donald Trump warned Iran could "cease to exist" if Washington is forced to escalate its military response.

Old video shows Siya leaning on Chetan as they watch cricket match togetherThe probe into Pune realtor Ketan Agarwal's murder has uncovered another link between accused Siya Goyal and Chetan Chaudhary, with police finding that the duo were seen together at an MFCL cricket match before the crime.

Venezuelan footballer's wife dies shielding daughter during catastrophic quakesHector Bello, who plays for the Martimo de La Guair club, announced on Instagram that his spouse, Andrea Bello, was killed on Wednesday, June 24, while protecting their 1-year-old daughter, Alana, during the devastating twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela earlier this week.

Cristiano Ronaldo's quiet night exposes Portugal's biggest World Cup problemFIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal were held 0-0 by Colombia as Cristiano Ronaldo was left chasing service in Miami. The draw sent Portugal through as runners-up but sharpened concerns over their midfield and identity before the Croatia clash.

Why men looking for sex reach Congress House in MumbaiMumbai's Congress House, the pre-Independence-era landmark, is not a brothel, but men driven by lust and desire often fail to make the distinction. The former Congress office's proximity to a mujra house and the red-light district of Kamathipura make it carry a stigma. However, it is not really untouched by the sex trade.

Live: Argentina, Austria and Algeria reach knockouts after epic final group dayJordan (JOR) vs Argentina (ARG) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi scored his 19th World Cup goal to help Argentina finish unbeaten in the group stage with a 3-1 win over Jordan while Austria equalise with the last kick of the game, sending both teams through to the round of 32 after one of the most insane games of World Cup football you will ever see. Iran is out.

Odisha woman rejects man's advances. He sets her house ablaze with her insideA woman in Sundargarh suffered severe burns after a man allegedly set her house on fire. Police have arrested the accused as investigators examine an alleged case of one-sided obsession.

Mumbai cops uncover Muharram poisoning plot, seize 14,900 rat poison capsulesMumbai Police detained a Pune businessman after seizing 14,900 capsules allegedly filled with zinc phosphide during a Muharram procession. Investigators are now examining his travels, finances and contacts for possible wider links.

India makes hydrogen from nuclear heat, not electricity. Why scientists are thrilledIndia has inaugurated the world's first plant that makes hydrogen using nuclear reactor heat instead of electricity. Built at Kalpakkam, the copper-chlorine cycle points to a cleaner, round-the-clock route to green hydrogen.

How Indira Gandhi's 'left turn' brought India-US ties under pressureFrom food aid and the 1966 rupee devaluation to the Bangladesh War, India's first nuclear test and the gradual revival of bilateral ties, Indira Gandhi's years in power marked one of the most turbulent phases in India-US relations. This article traces how Cold War politics, domestic challenges and shifting global alliances shaped New Delhi's relationship with Washington.
Telangana man, 25, studying in UK found dead after attending birthday bashThe victim has been identified as S Srinath Reddy, and he had moved to London around 14 months ago.

Bengaluru Court summons Priyank Kharge, Mohammed Nalpad in RSS defamation caseA Bengaluru court has taken cognisance of a private defamation complaint against Priyank Kharge and Mohammed Nalpad over alleged remarks on the RSS. The order starts criminal proceedings against the two leaders while dropping the complaint against Dinesh Gundu Rao.

India now dominates global research paper retractions with 6 universities in top 10India's rise in global research rankings is now shadowed by a sharp spike in paper retractions. With six Indian universities among the world's top 10 for retractions, concerns around plagiarism, fake peer reviews, and flawed incentives are growing, raising questions about credibility, funding, and the future of Indian science.

Monsoon arrived but India stayed dry: How 5 weather systems caused June's rain deficitIndia recorded one of its driest Junes in 146 years as the southwest monsoon stalled despite arriving on time. The rains have since revived in some regions, but the science of the stall reveals why much of the country stayed dry.

US hits Iran again, targets military sites in fresh strikes after ship attackThe latest action marks the second consecutive day of US military operations linked to attacks on commercial shipping in the region and represents the sharpest deterioration in relations since Washington and Tehran agreed to a memorandum of understanding.

After CBSE tender probe, Sarthak Siddant launches 1.66 crore govt record portalClass 12 student Sarthak Sidhant has launched a public portal carrying around 1.66 crore procurement records from the CPP Portal. He says the database is meant to help citizens, journalists and researchers examine government contracts more easily.

Harry Styles collapses on stage after choking while drinking water during concertHarry Styles suffered a brief choking scare after accidentally choking while drinking water during his Wembley Stadium concert in London. He collapsed on stage for a few seconds before recovering.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

How Jeffrey Epstein used a fake office to abuse women while serving jail termAn investigation by The Telegraph reveals how Jeffrey Epstein allegedly used a sham science foundation office during work release to maintain his lifestyle, host associates and continue abusing women despite being behind bars.

Siya, Chetan searched murder methods on Google, rehearsed how to push Ketan: CopsA rehearsal at the fort, online searches on murder methods, a disguise plan and deleted chats - investigators claim the accused left little to chance in the days leading up to Ketan Agarwal's death.

They are millionaires at 19. What will Gen Z be by 35?Handcrafted ByOur Editors A crore before 20. A managerial role while still a teenager. A startup before college. For a growing number of young Indians, career milestones once associated with the mid-30s are arriving years, even decades, earlier. But if success now starts at 19, what will life look like when they are 35?

TET paper leak mastermind made explosive claims in 2024 India Today stingMaharashtra Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) paper leak: Even as the Maharashtra Police search for the alleged mastermind, Bijendra Gupta, an India Today sting operation conducted in 2024 after the alleged NEET-UG paper leak has once again come into focus.

Lashkar terrorists, its deputy chief attend funeral of Shoaib Akhtar's brotherA viral video shows Saifullah Kasuri, deputy chief of the Lashkar-e-Taiba, and other terrorists at Shahid Akhtar's funeral in Islamabad. Shahid, the elder brother of Pakistani great Shoaib Akhtar, passed away earlier this week.

Jana Nayagan producer appointed Tamil Nadu Special Representative to DelhiTamil Nadu has appointed K Venkata Narayana as its Special Representative in New Delhi for one year. The move has drawn opposition criticism over his film industry background and proximity to C Joseph Vijay.

sports.headline: World Cup: Full Round of 32 matches decided after epic finish to group stage The FIFA World Cup Round of 32 matches have been finalised after a thrilling final day of the group stage. The final two places in the knockouts were decided after Algeria and Austria played out an epic 3-3 thriller.

Are India equipped enough to script another World Cup classic against Australia? Women's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Harmanpreet Kaur's India will take on Sophie Molineux's Australia in a blockbuster Group A clash at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground on Sunday.

2nd T20I: Will India unleash Sooryavanshi in search for a series-saving win? Ireland vs India, 2nd T20I preview: Stunned by a historic Irish victory in the Belfast opener, a fumbling Indian side faces a defining selection dilemma as Shreyas Iyer seeks to protect a proud three-year unbeaten T20I series streak.

Cristiano Ronaldo's quiet night exposes Portugal's biggest World Cup problem FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal were held 0-0 by Colombia as Cristiano Ronaldo was left chasing service in Miami. The draw sent Portugal through as runners-up but sharpened concerns over their midfield and identity before the Croatia clash.

DR Congo end 52-year wait with historic win, set up England clash in last 32 FIFA World Cup: DR Congo booked a historic place in the knockout stage and set up a round-of-32 clash with England after producing a stirring comeback from a goal down to beat Uzbekistan 3-1 in Atlanta on Saturday.

Harry Kane rewrites England World Cup history, overtakes Ronaldo in goals tally Harry Kane etched his name deeper into England's football history after becoming the country's highest goalscorer in FIFA World Cup history during the Three Lions' 2-0 win over Panama on Sunday.

Live: Argentina, Austria and Algeria reach knockouts after epic final group day Jordan (JOR) vs Argentina (ARG) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi scored his 19th World Cup goal to help Argentina finish unbeaten in the group stage with a 3-1 win over Jordan while Austria equalise with the last kick of the game, sending both teams through to the round of 32 after one of the most insane games of World Cup football you will ever see. Iran is out.

Thomas Tuchel gets the job done as Jude Bellingham downs stubborn Panama Panama vs England, FIFA World Cup 2026: Jude Bellingham starred with a goal and an assist as England beat Panama 2-0 to top Group L at the FIFA World Cup. Harry Kane became England's all-time leading World Cup scorer as Croatia also advanced.

COL vs POR Highlights: Portugal survive Colombia after 0-0 draw Colombia (COL) vs Portugal (POR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Diogo Costa's string of stunning saves helped Portugal survive a relentless Colombian assault, but it was Colombia who topped Group K after a goalless draw in Miami. DR Congo's win over Uzbekistan ensured all three sides progressed to the Round of 32 from a dramatic finale.

Cody Gakpo stays with Netherlands squad after heartbreaking loss of unborn son FIFA World Cup 2026: Cody Gakpo's World Cup journey has been overshadowed by unimaginable personal tragedy after the Netherlands forward and partner Noa van der Bij announced the loss of their unborn son. Despite the heartbreak, Gakpo has chosen to remain with the Dutch squad for the knockout stages.

DR Congo's Lumumba fan denied US visa for must-win clash vs Uzbekistan DR Congo's biggest match at the FIFA World Cup 2026 will be missing one of its most recognisable supporters. Cult fan Michel Kuka Mboladinga, famous for his Patrice Lumumba tribute, has been denied a US visa and will miss Saturday's crucial clash against Uzbekistan.

PAN 0-2 ENG: England and Croatia make it through to the knockoutsPanama vs England Live score, FIFA World Cup 2026: England sealed top spot in Group L with a 2-0 win over Panama as Jude Bellingham and Harry Kane struck, while Croatia beat Ghana 2-1 to join the Three Lions in the World Cup Round of 32.

Rachin Ravindra stars as Day 3 swings New Zealand's way in series deciderRachin Ravindra's unbeaten 60 guided New Zealand to a 204-run lead over England on Day 3 of the series decider at Trent Bridge, putting the Black Caps firmly in control heading into Day 4.

Sophie Molineux enjoying added captaincy role in Australia's dominant campaignWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Speaking to the reporters before the match against India, Sophie Molineux said that the ongoing tournament has been different, but she has enjoyed the added responsibility of captaincy after replacing Alyssa Healy.

Smriti Mandhana urges India to bat with more intent in must-win Australia clashWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Speaking to the reports, Smriti Mandhana said that India need to show more intent with the way if they are to beat Sophie Molineux's Australia on Sunday and qualify for the semis.

Ireland claim first-ever win in Women's T20 World Cup day after men's India upsetIreland Women created history by registering their first-ever Women's T20 World Cup victory, defeating West Indies by six wickets to end a 21-match losing streak, just a day after the Ireland men's team stunned India in Bristol.

Ex-Pakistan captain Sana Mir slams sexist remarks at women's teamFormer Pakistan captain Sana Mir has slammed the sexist backlash against the women's cricket team after its Women's T20 World Cup exit, urging fans to replace gender-based attacks with constructive criticism.

Ancy Sojan breaks Anju Bobby George's long jump national recordAncy Sojan shattered Anju Bobby George's 22-year-old women's long jump national record with a historic 6.88m leap at the National Inter State Senior Athletics Championships on Saturday.

Sarvesh Kushare smashes high jump national record, books Asian Games berthSarvesh Kushare set a new men's high jump national record by clearing 2.31m to win gold at the National Inter-State Athletics Championships in Bhubaneswar, eclipsing Tejaswin Shankar's eight-year-old mark of 2.29m.

India under pressure, Australia ready to make their life tougher: Sophie MolineuxWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Australia captain Sophie Molineux said her side will look to capitalise on the pressure India are under in Sunday's crucial Group A clash at Lord's Cricket Ground.

No money for equipment, Sindhushree breaks national record with borrowed poleG Sindhushree broke the national pole vault record with a borrowed pole to win gold and seal an Asian Games berth. She dedicated the achievement to her late father, who had backed her sporting journey.

India will draw confidence vs Australia from recent T20I series win: Shafali VermaWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Shafali Verma believes India will draw confidence from their recent 2-1 T20I series victory over Australia on Australian soil going into the Lord's clash.

Sooryavanshi winked at me, promised 13 sixes: Sangakkara reveals stunning IPL storyKumar Sangakkara recalled Vaibhav Sooryavanshi winking and predicting 13 sixes before a crucial IPL chase. The anecdote captured the 15-year-old's unusual self-belief as he moves closer to an India debut.

Naomi Osaka suffers injury scare before Wimbledon, retires from Bad Homburg finalNaomi Osaka's Wimbledon preparations suffered a major setback after she retired injured from the Bad Homburg Open final, handing Karolina Muchova the title just two days before the Grand Slam begins.

No one helps us: Furious Iran captain Taremi brands FIFA World Cup a 'disaster'Iran captain Mehdi Taremi tore into FIFA and its president, Gianni Infantino, after his side's World Cup campaign was hampered by travel restrictions and logistical problems, saying the tournament had been a "disaster" for Iran.

Badminton: Rounak Chouhan continues dream run, reaches semifinals of US OpenQualifier Rounak Chouhan beat Misha Zilberman to enter the US Open 2026 semi-finals. His surge followed a straight-games upset of top seed Chou Tien Chen, raising prospects of an all-Indian final.

From 1% hope to history: Cape Verde's World Cup dream refuses to endCape Verde, the side representing an archipelago of 10 islands in the Atlantic Ocean, arrived at the World Cup with just a one per cent chance of reaching the knockout stage. Three disciplined performances later, the Blue Sharks had become the smallest nation ever to reach the World Cup knockout rounds.

Missing hurdle: Teen athlete forced to run solo after shocking blunder from officialsInter-state Athletics Championships 2026: A shocking track blunder saw technical officials place only nine hurdles instead of 10, cruelly forcing Tamil Nadu sprinter Harshitha into a gruelling, lonely solo re-run that ultimately ended her hopes of making it to the final of the 400m hurdles event.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
